<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- At 22 files with perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling, this is the broadest and most authoritative test layer in the project — it enforces spawn-only, gateway, and tier-boundary rules that no unit test can replicate.
- architecture-policy.test.js and domain-isolation.test.js are designated 'required' in CLAUDE.md and must never be skipped — they are the last line of defense for the four-tier dependency hierarchy.
- The suite covers CLI delegation, error handling, config, MCP transport, integration coverage policy, and dev-mode startup — comprehensive horizontal coverage across all orchestration entry points.
- cli-dev.test.js and the scheduler startup test are explicitly flagged as sole coverage for their scenarios — document this in the test file headers so future contributors do not treat them as redundant.
- 22 e2e test files with zero cross-zone coupling means the suite never imports from production code directly, relying on subprocess invocation — this is the correct spawn-only testing pattern for orchestration-tier validation.
- The suite's scope (architecture policy, domain isolation, MCP transport, integration coverage) makes it a living contract document; consider adding a brief top-of-file comment to each test file naming which policy or contract it owns.
- The e2e suite's zero-coupling guarantee relies on spawn-based CLI invocation rather than direct imports — this means the suite observes only exit codes and stdout/stderr, not in-process state; subtle in-process bugs (e.g. PRDStore partial corruption) are invisible here and must be caught by unit or integration tests, making the three-layer test architecture (unit, integration, e2e) load-bearing rather than redundant.
- The e2e suite enforces the spawn-only contract by never importing from production code — this self-referential property means the suite cannot accidentally violate the architectural rule it validates, making it the most stable test zone in the monorepo and a model for future policy enforcement additions.
- cli-dev.test.js already contains a required-test protection comment (lines 1-10) with explicit guidance against removal — this is the correct pattern; the existing insight claiming it lacks in-file protection was inaccurate and should be disregarded.
- The e2e zone metadata records 22 files but the current filesystem contains 20 test files in tests/e2e/. Zone metadata is 2 files stale — either 2 test files were removed or renamed after the last zone analysis. Stale file counts cause cohesion metrics to be computed against an incorrect denominator, making the reported cohesion of 1.0 technically unreliable until the zone is re-analyzed against the current file set.
- e2e zone metadata reports 22 files but only 20 .test.js files exist in tests/e2e/ — a 2-file discrepancy indicating zone metadata was not refreshed after files were removed or renamed. Any automated zone health checks that use the stored file count for cohesion/coupling validation will report against a stale baseline. Re-run zone analysis to synchronize metadata with the current filesystem.
